step_text: We admitted we were powerless over alcohol — that our lives had become unmanageable.
principle_body: Honesty: telling the truth about what we are and what alcohol has done to us, without softening it.

step_text: Came to believe that a Power greater than ourselves could restore us to sanity.
principle_body: Hope: the willingness to believe that recovery is possible, even before we can prove it.

step_text: Made a decision to turn our will and our lives over to the care of God as we understood Him.
principle_body: Faith: making the decision to act as if a Power greater than ourselves is caring for us, one day at a time.

step_text: Made a searching and fearless moral inventory of ourselves.
principle_body: Courage: putting our resentments, fears, and harms on paper, naming them so they lose their power over us.

step_text: Admitted to God, to ourselves, and to another human being the exact nature of our wrongs.
principle_body: Integrity: speaking the inventory aloud to another human being, holding nothing back.

step_text: Were entirely ready to have God remove all these defects of character.
principle_body: Willingness: becoming entirely ready, even when we still find some defect comfortable.

step_text: Humbly asked Him to remove our shortcomings.
principle_body: Humility: asking, not demanding; right-sizing ourselves before our Higher Power.

step_text: Made a list of all persons we had harmed, and became willing to make amends to them all.
principle_body: Brotherly Love: looking honestly at the harms we caused and becoming willing to set them right.

step_text: Made direct amends to such people wherever possible, except when to do so would injure them or others.
principle_body: Justice: making the amend, in person where we can, accepting the consequences either way.

step_text: Continued to take personal inventory and when we were wrong promptly admitted it.
principle_body: Perseverance: cleaning house at the end of every day, promptly admitting it when we were wrong.

step_text: Sought through prayer and meditation to improve our conscious contact with God, as we understood Him, praying only for knowledge of His will for us and the power to carry that out.
principle_body: Spirituality: prayer and meditation as a daily practice — asking only for knowledge of His will and the power to carry it out.

step_text: Having had a spiritual awakening as the result of these Steps, we tried to carry this message to alcoholics, and to practice these principles in all our affairs.
principle_body: Service: giving away what we were given, practicing these principles in all our affairs.
